channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Venice extends its day-tripper tax through next year to combat overtourism
item.description: ROME (AP) — Venice will extend its day-tripper tax through next year, increasing the number of days on which tourists have to pay to enter the city and doubling the fee to 10 euros ($10.80) for last-minute visitors, city officials said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party is elected as speaker of Austria's parliament
item.description: A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party has been elected as the new speaker of Austria’s parliament, weeks after the party won a national election for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Paris conference for Lebanon raises $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian and military support
item.description: PARIS (AP) — An international conference for Lebanon in Paris on Thursday raised $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian aid and military support to help the country where war between Hezbollah militants and Israel has displaced a million people, killed over 2,500, and deepened an economic crisis, Fr
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US announces $135M in humanitarian assistance to Palestinians
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Thursday announced $135 million in new humanitarian assistance for Palestinians and said American negotiators will meet with Israeli and Qatari counterparts to revive talks on a cease-fire despite uncertainty over Hamas’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: In Colombia, Amazon River's extreme drought falls hard on Indigenous communities
item.description: The severe drought of the Amazon River has caused Indigenous communities who live beside it to struggle for food, water and simply getting from one place to another.
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin presides over BRICS summit seeking to expand Russia's clout
item.description: KAZAN, Russia (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Thursday presided at the closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, praising its role as a counterbalance to what he called the West's “perverse methods.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venice extends its day-tripper tax through next year to combat overtourism
item.description: Venice will extend its day-tripper tax through next year, increasing the number of days and doubling the fee to 10 euros for last-minute visitors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Evangelicals emerge as a potent lobby for US support of Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian evangelicals are lobbying their American counterparts for continued support of their homeland in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tropical storm battering Philippines leaves at least 24 people dead in flooding and landslides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Widespread flooding and landslides set off by a tropical storm in the northeastern Philippines on Thursday left at least 24 people dead, swept away cars and prompted authorities to scramble for motorboats to rescue trapped villagers, some on roofs.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian lawmakers on Thursday ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Well-known Cambodian investigative journalist released on bail
item.description: TA KHMAU, Cambodia (AP) — A Cambodian investigative reporter who was charged with a criminal offense after exposing online scams and corruption was released on bail Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president raises possibility of supplying Ukraine with arms
item.description: South Korea’s president has raised the possibility of supplying Ukraine with weapons while stressing that his government “won’t sit idle” as North Korea allegedly sends troops to support Russia’s aggression toward its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man pleads guilty to displaying a Nazi symbol at pro-Palestinian rally
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — A restaurateur pleaded guilty Thursday to displaying a Nazi symbol during a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney in early October. Alan Yazbek was photographed at the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel accuses 6 Al Jazeera journalists in Gaza of being Palestinian militants
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The Israeli army on Wednesday accused six Al Jazeera journalists covering the war in Gaza of also being current or former paid fighters for Palestinian militant groups. Al Jazeera rejected the claims.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria following deadly attack on defense company
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey’s air force struck Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria on Wednesday in an apparent retaliation for an attack at a key state-run defense company that killed five people and wounded more than 20 others.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's population has fallen by 10 million since Russia's invasion, UN says
item.description: The United Nations said Ukraine's population has decreased by around a quarter, or 10 million people, since Russia's invasion of the country in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Situation in Haiti worsens amid ‘acute violence’ as UN support mission falters under gang violence
item.description: The gang violence in Haiti has gotten worse despite the deployment of U.N.-sanctioned international police officers, forcing mass displacement, hunger among millions and even famine for thousands of Haitians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah leader's expected successor killed in Israeli airstrike
item.description: Hezbollah on Wednesday confirmed one of its top officials, expected to be the group’s next leader, was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: North Korea has sent troops to Russia, Pentagon confirms
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin confirmed that North Korean troops have been transferred to Russia on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Floods in Philippines kill at least 9 and trap others on roofs as storm approaches
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Torrential rain set off by an approaching tropical storm swamped the eastern Philippines with widespread flooding that killed at least nine people, trapped others on their roofs and sparked frantic appeals for help, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah confirms death of senior commander in line to replace Hassan Nasrallah
item.description: The Israeli military eliminated yet another top commander within Hezbollah this month, as Secretary of State Antony Blinken met with Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanders leads call for DOJ to investigate Israeli attack on journalists
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and 11 Democrats are urging President Biden, Secretary of State Antony Blinken and Attorney General Merrick Garland to open a formal investigation into an Israeli airstrike that injured American journalists more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: Police said they haven't yet confirmed the cause and manner of death of the woman, who worked at the store, nor has her name been released.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Woman wedged between boulders in Australia for hours
item.description: The woman had apparently dropped her phone and was trying to retrieve it while hiking in Australia, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, NewsNation

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken arrives in Israel as Biden-Harris admin faces scrutiny over compromising Jerusalem’s security
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken arrived for talks with Israel on his 11th trip there since October 2023. The Jewish state is fighting a seven-front war against Iran-backed proxies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China holds live-fire drills near Taiwan, in another show of force
item.description: China's military is conducting live-fire drills around islands near Taiwan Tuesday that are being tracked by the Taiwan Defense Ministry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian surfer dies in freak accident after swordfish impales her while surfing in Indonesia
item.description: An Italian surfer hunting for waves off the west coast of Sumatra died after she was stabbed in the chest by the bill of a swordfish, in what is being considered a freak accident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paul Whelan describes 'torture' of Russian prison
item.description: Former Marine Paul Whelan described the “torture” he endured while being held captive in a Russian prison for years. “The Russian government wanted to put pressure on the United States by treating me badly. Sleep deprivation is considered torture,” Whelan said Sunday on CBS’s “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation
